Diary — Introverting

Shooting the moment before it's over.

Shinya Miyakoshi started shooting local cards eight years ago with a single body and a cheap 70-200, sneaking as close to the cage as security would allow. What started as a way to get free tickets turned into a full-time practice — covering everything from amateur BJJ tournaments to televised title fights.

The work is built around timing, not luck. A fight is decided in a handful of frames: the setup, the shift in weight, the moment a corner sees it before the crowd does. That's the frame worth chasing.

Disciplines

Available for event coverage, fighter portraits, promotional stills, and fight week documentary work. Based on the road, available for travel to any card.

Diary — Browsing

Notes from the road.

A running log of loose thoughts, behind-the-scenes bits, and things worth remembering from fight week — the kind of material that doesn't fit neatly into a portfolio grid but is still part of the story.

This page is a placeholder for that ongoing log. Drop in dated entries, quick photo dumps, or short writeups here as they come.
